summary: Security policy for Microsoft account usage on the device. The local Microsoft security option metadata explicitly documents the same registry path, value name, and allowed values that the app uses.

setting.name: Microsoft Accounts on This Device
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can let people add Microsoft accounts or sign in with them on the device. This security option controls whether Microsoft accounts are allowed, partly blocked, or fully blocked.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change this on kiosks, shared devices, lab systems, or privacy-focused PCs where they want to keep the machine local-account-only.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Blocking Microsoft accounts increases local control, but it can prevent account linking, Microsoft account sign-in, and other consumer features that depend on Microsoft identity.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents this security option through LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions. The registry mapping is HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Policies\System\NoConnectedUser with allowed values 0, 1, and 3.

allowed_values.label: Microsoft accounts allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The security option is disabled and users can add or sign in with Microsoft accounts normally.

allowed_values.label: Block adding new Microsoft accounts
allowed_values.meaning: Users cannot add new Microsoft accounts, but the stronger sign-in block is not enforced.

allowed_values.label: Block adding and signing in with Microsoft accounts
allowed_values.meaning: Users cannot add Microsoft accounts and cannot sign in with them on the device.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes NoConnectedUser = 3, which matches the fully blocked Microsoft-account state documented by Microsoft.

windows_defaults.label: Windows security-option default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows client devices that expose the Accounts: Block Microsoft accounts security option

states.rationale: Microsoft documents the effective default value as 0.

windows_defaults.notes: The official LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions documentation lists 0 as the default effective value for this setting.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that use Microsoft accounts normally
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the normal Windows consumer account experience.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to prevent Microsoft account usage on the device.
tradeoffs: Microsoft account sign-in and linking remain available.
No identity restriction is enforced by this security option.

recommended_profiles.label: No new Microsoft accounts
intended_for: Systems that should not add new cloud identities casually
avoid_for: Personal devices where users may need to link a Microsoft account

states.rationale: Block adding new Microsoft accounts without selecting the strongest sign-in restriction.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want a lighter restriction than a full sign-in block.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: Users need to add Microsoft accounts freely.
tradeoffs: Adding new Microsoft accounts is restricted.
This is still a policy-enforced identity restriction.

recommended_profiles.label: Local-only sign-in
avoid_for: Devices that rely on Microsoft account sign-in

states.rationale: Use the strongest documented Microsoft-account block.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to stop Microsoft accounts from being added or used to sign in.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: Users need Microsoft account-based sign-in, syncing, or account linking.
tradeoffs: Consumer account-based workflows can stop working as expected.

evidence.title: Microsoft LocalPoliciesSecurityOptions CSP: Accounts_Block_Microsoft_accounts
evidence.summary: Microsoft documents the registry path, registry value name, allowed values 0, 1, and 3, and the default effective value for this security option.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes NoConnectedUser = 3 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak provenance record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: The local Microsoft security option metadata in sceregvl.inf explicitly defines NoConnectedUser under Policies\System with allowed values 0, 1, and 3, and the app writes one of those documented values.

validation_proof.notes: The local official Microsoft security option metadata names the same registry path and value used by the app and exposes the documented 0, 1, and 3 states.
